originalTitle: Russian mercenaries to withdraw from Mali city after attacks
title: Big News from Mali: Helpers Are Leaving Kidal!
content:
Hello from Sunny Africa!

Hello, super kids! I'm your friendly news reporter, here to tell you about something happening in a very special country called Mali. Mali is in our wonderful continent of Africa, just like us! It's a land of sunshine, amazing music, and super friendly people. Today, we have some important news from a city in Mali called Kidal. Sometimes, grown-ups have big problems, and they try to solve them. This news is about some people who were helping, and now they are getting ready to go home. We are going to talk about "helpers" from a far-off country. These helpers were in Kidal. But now, they are going to leave. Let's find out why, in a simple way!

What Happened in Kidal?

Imagine your favorite playground. Sometimes, friends might have a small disagreement, right? Well, in Kidal, there was some bigger trouble. There were "clashes." This means some groups of people were fighting each other. Fighting is never fun. It makes people sad and scared. This fighting happened in Kidal, and also in other parts of Mali. It happened on a Saturday, not too long ago. When there are fights, things can get tricky. People want everyone to be safe and happy again. That's why we always wish for peace. Did you know? Kidal is a city in the northern part of Mali. It's a place with a long history and beautiful desert landscapes!

Who Are the "Helpers from Far Away"?

Sometimes, when a country has big problems or trouble, other countries might send people to help. Or, a country might hire people to help with security. These people are called "mercenaries." Think of them like special guards or soldiers, but they are not regular army people from Mali. They are "for hire." These particular helpers were from a country very, very far away. Can you guess? They were from Russia! Russia is a huge country, many miles away from Africa. These Russian helpers came to Mali to help the government keep things safe. They were working to make sure bad things didn't happen. Did you know? Russia is the largest country in the world! It's so big that it crosses two continents, Europe and Asia.

Why Are the Russian Helpers Leaving Kidal?

So, why are these helpers from Russia now packing their bags in Kidal? Well, remember we talked about the "clashes" and "attacks"? There were new troubles and some groups of people with weapons started fighting again in Mali. When there is a lot of fighting, it can be very difficult for everyone, including the helpers. Because of these new troubles, the Russian helpers have decided it's time for them to leave Kidal. It's like when a guest leaves after helping you with a big task. Their job in Kidal is coming to an end. Did you know? When there are fights, sometimes people try to talk to each other to solve problems. This is called diplomacy, and it's a very important way to find peace!

Mali: A Wonderful Country

Let's remember how amazing Mali is! It's a big country in West Africa. Imagine a place with lots of sunshine, some big sandy deserts, and also rivers where people live and farm. Mali is famous for its incredible music. People play beautiful instruments like the kora and sing wonderful songs. It's also known for its rich history and ancient cities like Timbuktu, which has very old and wise libraries. The people of Mali are known for being very kind and welcoming. They love their country very much and want it to be a peaceful place for everyone, especially for all the children. Did you know? The Great Mosque of Djenné in Mali is the largest mud-brick building in the world! It's super old and very famous.

Who Are the "Armed Groups"?

We talked about "armed groups" causing trouble. These are groups of people who have guns and sometimes cause problems or fight. They are not part of the government's army. Sometimes, these groups have different ideas about how things should be in Mali. When people have different ideas, it's always best to talk and listen to each other, like you do with your friends. Fighting with guns is never the answer. It only brings sadness and makes it harder for everyone to live happily. We hope that one day, all these groups will put down their weapons and talk instead. Did you know? Many countries around the world work together to help bring peace to places that have trouble. It's like many hands working to build a better future!

Why Peace is So Important for Mali

The most important thing for Mali, and for every country, is peace! What does peace mean? It means no fighting, no shouting, and no one being scared. When there is peace, children can go to school every day and learn new things. They can play outside with their friends without worrying. Families can live happily in their homes. Peace means everyone can grow food, build houses, and help their community get stronger. It means laughter, kindness, and a bright future for all the kids in Mali. Did you know? The symbol for peace is often a dove, a beautiful white bird. It flies freely and represents harmony and calm.

What Happens Next for Mali?

So, the Russian helpers are leaving Kidal. This is a big change. But the people of Mali are strong and hopeful. They want to find their own way to make their country safe and peaceful. They will keep working hard to make sure everyone can live together without fighting. It might take time, like growing a big, strong tree from a tiny seed. We wish all the children in Mali a future filled with peace, happiness, and lots of laughter. We hope they can go to school, play with friends, and chase their dreams without any worries. Did you know? Many children around the world learn about peace in their schools. They learn how to share, be kind, and solve problems without fighting!

Wishing for a Peaceful Future

So, that's our news from Mali today. Some helpers are leaving, but the spirit of Mali remains strong. Everyone in Mali wants peace, and we hope they find it very soon. Let's all send happy thoughts and wishes for peace to our friends in Mali. Remember, even small acts of kindness can help make the world a more peaceful place!
summary: In the African country of Mali, some special helpers from Russia are leaving a city called Kidal after new fighting started, and everyone hopes for peace to return soon.
whyMatters: This is important because when helpers leave, people in Mali need to work even harder to find peace and safety so that children can go to school and play happily without worries.

title: Kenya's Sawe smashes two-hour barrier to make history at London Marathon
content: Sabastian Sawe makes history at the London Marathon as the first person to run a sub-two-hour marathon in a competitive race.

originalTitle: The detained anti-colonial activist grabbing attention in West Africa: Who is Kemi Seba?
title: A Big Voice for Africa: Who is Kemi Seba?
content:
Hey there, awesome explorers of Africa! Today, we're going to talk about a man named Kemi Seba. He's making lots of people talk in West Africa, and we want to understand why. It's like when someone has a very strong idea and wants to share it with everyone! Let's learn about him together.

Who is Kemi Seba?

Kemi Seba is a person who cares a lot about Africa. He was born in a country called France, but his family is from Benin, which is a country in West Africa. He is what we call an 'activist'. This means he works very hard to make changes he believes are good for people. Imagine if you really wanted to make your school playground better, and you talked to everyone about it – that's a bit like being an activist, but for whole countries! Kemi Seba travels around West Africa. He gives speeches and talks to many people. He wants to help African countries be super strong and make all their own decisions. He believes Africa should shine very brightly, all on its own.

What Does 'Anti-Colonial' Mean?

This is a big word, but let's make it simple. Long, long ago, some strong countries from Europe came to Africa. They took over many African lands and made them their 'colonies'. It was like a big brother telling a younger brother exactly what to do, all the time. The African people living there did not always get to choose their own leaders or make their own rules. 'Anti-colonial' means you don't like this idea of one country bossing another country around. Kemi Seba is 'anti-colonial' because he believes Africa should be completely free. He wants African countries to be in charge of their own money, their own schools, and their own future. He wants them to be truly independent. Did you know? Most African countries became independent, or free, many years ago, often in the 1950s and 1960s! They raised their own flags and chose their own leaders.

France's Influence in West Africa

Even though many African countries are independent, Kemi Seba and others say that some European countries, especially France, still have a lot of 'influence'. What does influence mean? It's like when your best friend always tells you what games to play, or what snacks to eat. Your friend isn't your boss, but they still have a big say in what you do. France used to be the 'colonial master' for many countries in West Africa. Today, these countries are free. But some people, like Kemi Seba, say France still has a lot of power. This can be about how money works, or who countries trade with, or even what language people speak. Did you know? French is still an official language in many West African countries because of the old colonial times! This helps people communicate, but also reminds some people of the past.

Why Kemi Seba Says 'Stop!'

Kemi Seba says that African countries should decide everything for themselves. He thinks that if France still has too much influence, African countries can't truly grow as strong as they could be. He talks a lot about the 'CFA franc', which is a type of money used in many West African countries. This money is connected to the French currency, and Kemi Seba believes African countries should have their own money that is totally separate from France. He wants African countries to be like a grown-up person who makes all their own decisions about their life, without someone else always telling them what to do. He believes this is the best way for African people to build a great future. Did you know? Just like you learn to make your own choices as you grow up, countries also want to make their own choices about their future!

What About Being a 'Russian Mouthpiece'?

This is another big idea! Sometimes, when someone speaks very strongly against one country, other countries might listen. Some people have said that Kemi Seba is a 'Russian mouthpiece'. This means they think he is speaking for Russia, or that Russia is telling him what to say. It's like if you had a friend who always said nice things about one teacher, and then someone else said, 'Oh, that friend is just a mouthpiece for the teacher!' It means they think your friend is speaking on behalf of someone else. Kemi Seba has visited Russia and talked to people there. Because of this, some people wonder if he is getting ideas or help from Russia to talk about France. Did you know? Countries sometimes become friends or allies, and they talk to each other a lot. But it's important for everyone to speak their own true mind.

Why is Kemi Seba in the News?

Kemi Seba is often in the news because he speaks very loudly and clearly about what he believes. Sometimes, when people speak out strongly, especially about big ideas like how countries should be run, they can get into trouble. The original news said he was 'detained', which means he was held by the police for a short time. This can happen to activists who make very strong statements or hold protests. He has been arrested in different countries in West Africa because of his protests. But even when he is detained, he keeps on sharing his ideas. Many people listen to him because they also want African countries to be strong and truly independent. His ideas are making many young people think about the future of Africa. Did you know? People all over the world have the right to speak up for what they believe is right. It's called 'freedom of speech', and it's very important!

Why is This Important?

Kemi Seba's story helps us understand that even when countries are independent, there can still be challenges. People like him want to make sure that African countries have total control over their own destiny. They want Africa to be a leader in the world, making its own choices and building a fantastic future for all its people. It's about empowering Africa to be the best it can be! So, Kemi Seba is a man with a big voice, asking big questions about how African countries can be even stronger and more independent. He wants Africa to truly shine! That's all for today, awesome adventurers. Keep exploring and learning!
